metadata.description: for 迭代原理: The given Chinese technical text translates to: "For `x` in `iterable`: is equivalent to calling `iter()` → `__next__()` until `StopIteration`. Here's a brief Python code example to illustrate this concept: ```python # Define an iterable my_list = [1, 2, 3] # Using the for loop for x in my_list: print(x) # Equivalent manual iteration using iter() and __next__() it = iter(my_list) # Get the iterator object try: while True: # Continue until StopIteration is raised print(next(it)) # This is equivalent to x in for loop except StopIteration: pass # End of iterable, do nothing ``` In this example, both the `for` loop and the manual iteration using `iter()` and `next()` achieve the same result."

data_pointers.title: for 迭代原理
data_pointers.content_preview: for 迭代原理: The given Chinese technical text translates to: "For `x` in `iterable`: is equivalent to calling `iter()` → `__next__()` until `StopIteration`. Here's a brief Python code example to illustrate this concept: ```python # Define an iterable my_list = [1, 2, 3] # Using the for loop for x in my_list: print(x) # Equivalent manual iteration using iter() and __next__() it = iter(my_list) # Get the iterator object try: while True: # Continue until StopIteration is raised print(next(it)) # This is equivalent to x in for loop except StopIteration: pass # End of iterable, do nothing ``` In this example, both the `for` loop and the manual iteration using `iter()` and `next()` achieve the same result."
